name: Archie Shepp
abstract: Archie Shepp (born May 24, 1937) is an American jazz saxophonist, educator and playwright who since the 1960s has played a central part in the development of avant-garde jazz.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Archie Shepp Quintet
Laura Nyro, The Rascals,  Alice Coltrane, and Pharoah Sanders
Newport Jazz Festival: Weather Report, Roy Haynes Hip Ensemble, Tony Williams's Lifetime, and the Archie Schepp Quintet

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: From that moment on, every job that Archie Shepp would get, he'd call me... we played up in Buffalo... That's the group that Archie Shepp took up... Archie had another gig. He had Carnegie Hall and he used me
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: From that moment on, every job that Archie Shepp would get, he'd call me... That's the group that Archie Shepp took up. No rehearsal. We just went up there and played.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Vi Redd
about_quotes.evidence: I worked two weeks opposite Ben Webster, then two weeks opposite Max Roach, then two weeks opposite Archie Shepp
about_quotes.doc_title: Vi Redd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER asks about reaction to music of Archie Shepp... INTERVIEWEE responds generally about liking experimentation and pushing edges, but does not mention a personal interaction or specific relationship with Shepp.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: When you want to talk about avant-garde, you’re talking about; Albert Ayler, Pharoah Saunders, or one phase of Archie Shepp... I think of some of these folks as authentic avant-garde players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: my alliances and allegiances, when I was going through this loft period, were with the avant garde, whether it was Archie Shepp or Bill Dixon or any of the guys who came out of that.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
